They have erred in this. Rather, it is a legal and social issue (حقوقية اجتماعية) stemming from the issue of kinship (القرابة). Nations and peoples differ in defining and identifying it: Is the woman included in kinship? Are the children of a man's daughter his children? Does kinship restrict itself to what occurs through birth, or does it encompass it and what occurs through adoption (الادعاء)? The Arabs of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) did not recognize for a woman anything but natural kinship (القرابة الطبيعية) which has its effect in marriage, maintenance, and the like; they did not recognize for her a legal kinship (قرابة قانونية) that would allow her inheritance and such. As for the children of daughters, they did not see any kinship for them. However, they did recognize the kinship of adopted children (الأدعياء) and called the adopted one a "son," not because the language permitted that, but because they followed the advanced nations neighboring them who saw that according to their civil laws or national customs, such as Rome and Persia.

As for Islam, it abolished the kinship of adopted children entirely. The Exalted said: **وَما جَعَلَ أَدْعِياءَكُمْ أَبْناءَكُمْ** (nor has He made your adopted sons your [real] sons. [33:4]). It included the woman in kinship and arranged its effects accordingly, and it included the children of daughters among the children (الأولاد). The Exalted said in the verse of inheritance: **يُوصِيكُمُ اللهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ** (Allah enjoins you concerning your children: for the male shall be the like of the share of two females. [4:11]). And He said: **لِلرِّجالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ وَلِلنِّساءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ** (Men have a share in what parents and near relatives leave, and women have a share in what parents and near relatives leave, whether it be little or much. [4:7]). And He said in the verse of prohibited marriages: **حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهاتُكُمْ وَبَناتُكُمْ** — until He said — **وَأُحِلَّ لَكُمْ ما وَراءَ ذلِكُمْ** (Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters... and lawful to you are [all women] beyond these. [4:23-24]). Thus, He called the daughter of a daughter a "daughter" and the children of daughters "children" without any doubt. And the Exalted said: **وَيَحْيى وَعِيسى وَإِلْياسَ**, counting Jesus among the progeny of Abraham or Noah (عليه السلام), though he is not connected to them except through the mother.

The Imams of the Household (عليه السلام) argued using this verse, the verse of prohibition, and the Verse of Mubahalah that the son of a man's daughter is his son. The evidence is general even if the argument was for a specific matter. Abu Ja'far al-Baqir (عليه السلام) has another argument more explicit than all of them, narrated in *al-Kafi* with his chain from Abd al-Samad ibn Bashir from Abu al-Jarud, who said: Abu Ja'far (عليه السلام) said: "O Abu al-Jarud, what do they say to you regarding al-Hasan and al-Husayn?" I said: "They deny to us that they are the two sons of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)." He said: "With what did you argue against them?" I said: "We argued against them with the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, regarding Jesus son of Mary: **وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ داوُدَ وَسُلَيْمانَ ـ وَأَيُّوبَ وَيُوسُفَ وَمُوسى وَهارُونَ ـ وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ وَزَكَرِيَّا وَيَحْيى وَعِيسى**, so He made Jesus son of Mary from the progeny of Noah."

He said: "What did they say to you?" I said: "They said: 'The child of a daughter may be from the children (الولد), but he is not from the loins (الصلب).'" He said: "With what did you argue against them?" I said: "We argued against them with His saying, the Exalted, to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): **فَقُلْ تَعالَوْا نَدْعُ أَبْناءَنا وَأَبْناءَكُمْ وَنِساءَنا وَنِساءَكُمْ ـ وَأَنْفُسَنا وَأَنْفُسَكُمْ**." Then he said: "What did they say?" I said: "They said: 'In the speech of the Arabs, there may be the sons of a man, and another says: Our sons.'"

He said: So Abu Ja'far (عليه السلام) said: "I shall certainly give you from the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, that they are from the loins of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), which none but a disbeliever would reject." I said: "And where is that, may I be your ransom?" He said: "From where Allah said: **حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهاتُكُمْ وَبَناتُكُمْ وَأَخَواتُكُمْ** until He reached His saying, Blessed and Exalted: **وَحَلائِلُ أَبْنائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلابِكُمْ** (and the spouses of your sons who are from your loins [4:23]). O Abu al-Jarud, would it have been lawful for the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) to marry their spouses? If they say 'Yes,' they have lied and committed an outrage; and if they say 'No,' then they are his sons from his loins." Al-Qummi narrated something similar in his *Tafsir*.

In short, the issue is not linguistic. Islam has considered in the woman both natural kinship — *meaning the kinship from which legal rulings follow without much burden in their establishment, such as the human's specific relation to what they have birthed and the right of custody* — and legislative kinship. Likewise, it considered the children of daughters to be among the children and that the pillar of lineage (عمود النسب) runs from the side of the woman just as it runs from the side of the man. It also abolished the connection of lineage through adoption or through other than a legal marriage. Both groups [Sunni and Shi'a] have narrated from him (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) that he said: "The child belongs to the bed, and for the fornicator is the stone." However, the negligence of people regarding religious truths has made them forget this reality, and nothing remains of it except some of its effects like inheritance and prohibition. The international powers in the early period of Islam were not without influence in this. The discussion regarding this preceded under the verse of prohibition in the third volume of this book.

In *Tafsir al-Nu'mani*, with his chain from Sulayman ibn Harun al-Ijli, who said: I heard Abu Abdillah (عليه السلام) saying: **إن صاحب هذا الأمر محفوظة له ـ لو ذهب الناس جميعا أتى الله بأصحابه، وهم الذين قال الله عز وجل : (فَإِنْ يَكْفُرْ بِها هؤُلاءِ ـ فَقَدْ وَكَّلْنا بِها قَوْماً لَيْسُوا بِها بِكافِرِينَ) وهم الذين قال الله فيهم : (فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ ـ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَعِزَّةٍ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ)** — "Indeed, the Master of this Affair (صاحب هذا الأمر) has his [companions] preserved for him. If all people were to go away, Allah would bring his companions, and they are those of whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'So if these disbelieve in them, We have certainly entrusted them to a people who are not disbelievers in them.' And they are those of whom Allah said: 'Allah will soon bring a people whom He loves and who love Him, [who will be] humble towards the believers, mighty against the faithless' [5:54]."

**I say:** This is of the nature of application (الجري).

In *al-Kafi*, with his chain from Abu Hamzah from Abu Ja'far (عليه السلام): Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said in His Book: **وَنُوحاً هَدَيْنا مِنْ قَبْلُ** until His saying **بِكافِرِينَ** — "For He entrusted the grace (الفضل) to his household, brothers, and progeny. And it is the saying of Allah, Blessed and Exalted: **فَإِنْ يَكْفُرْ بِها** — 'If your nation disbelieves in it, We have entrusted your household with the faith with which We sent you, so they will never disbelieve in it. And I shall not let the faith with which I sent you be lost from your household after you—the scholars of your nation, the guardians of My affair after you, and the people of derivation (استنباط) of knowledge in which there is no lie, no sin, no burden, no insolence, and no showing off.'"

**I say:** Al-Ayyashi narrated it without a chain, as he did the one before it, and the hadith, like the one preceding it, is of the nature of application (الجري).

In *al-Mahasin*, with his chain from Ali ibn Uyaynah from Abu Abdillah (عليه السلام), who said: Abu Abdillah (عليه السلام) said: "I entered upon Abu al-Abbas while the people had taken their seats. He extended his hand to me while the dining cloth (السفرة) was placed before him. I went to step toward him and my foot fell on the edge of the dining cloth. Because of that, there entered into me what Allah willed should enter [of shame], for Allah says: **فَإِنْ يَكْفُرْ بِها هؤُلاءِ ـ فَقَدْ وَكَّلْنا بِها قَوْماً لَيْسُوا بِها بِكافِرِينَ** — a people, by Allah, who establish prayer, give zakat, and remember Allah much."

**I say:** The gist of it is his (عليه السلام) feeling of shame before Allah, the Glorified, because his foot fell on the edge of the dining cloth out of necessity, as if treading on the dining cloth were an ingratitude (كفران) for the blessing of Allah. In this is a generalization of disbelief (الكفر) in His saying "are not disbelievers in them" (لَيْسُوا بِها بِكافِرِينَ) to include ingratitude for blessings (كفر النعمة).

In *al-Nahj*: **اقتدوا بهدى نبيكم فإنه أفضل الهدى** — "Follow the guidance of your Prophet, for it is the best of guidance."

**I say:** Its derivation from the verses is manifest.

In *Tafsir al-Qummi*, from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), who said: **وأحسن الهدى هدى الأنبياء** — "And the best of guidance is the guidance of the prophets."
